description:
Third post-implementation phase of bl-f021. Documentation sweep that makes the XDG three-file layout the documented *default*, demotes legacy-layout text to historical/migration sections, and drafts the 0.5.0 release notes. Depends on Phase 6 (bl-cf5f) so that any surprises found by the integration sweep are reflected in the docs before they ship.

## Scope

### README.md

- File/Folder Layout diagram: replace the in-tree `.balls/` tree with the XDG three-tree layout. Bare-hub variant updated to match.
- Quick-start: `bl init` post-XDG produces no `.balls/` directory on main; the diagram and walkthrough text agree.
- 'Federated multi-repo state' (bl-7cea) and 'Persistent cross-repo task-master via committed master_url' (bl-ffb4) sections rewritten to describe the redirect-via-`tracker.json` mechanism instead of the old `master_url` / `state_remote` distinction. One mechanism, no modes.
- 'Delivery Modes' (bl-5305) refers to `integrate.mode` / `direct` / `forge-pr` (Phase 2 renames).
- 'Forge-gated delivery' SPEC reference uses `review.gate_command` not `review.pre_check`.
- Legacy-layout migration section: short, points at `bl prime --migrate` as the action, links to the §11 migration matrix in SPEC-clone-layout.

### AGENTS.md / SKILL.md

- New commands documented: `bl migrate`, `bl prime --migrate`, `bl repair --rebind-path`.
- 'Operating against a bare hub' section: paths updated to XDG; the 'cd to repo root' rule for `bl close` clarified for the case where the worktree lives outside the code repo.
- 'Multi-repo: one project, many repos' section: redirect mechanism replaces `master_url`/`state_remote` text. The 'master wins' precedence rule survives; the wording references the hub's `tracker.json` instead of `master_url`.
- Error Recovery table: add the `bl doctor` moved-clone hint + `bl repair --rebind-path` row.

### SPEC sweep

- SPEC-clone-layout.md: any §11 migration text written in future tense flips to past tense ('migrate' → 'migrated').
- SPEC-tracker-state.md: confirm fc50 terminology sweep + this phase's rename ridealong leave no stale references to `delivery`/`pre_check`/`workspace.json`.
- SPEC-forge-gated-delivery.md: confirm Phase 2's cross-doc rename ridealong (bl-717e) actually landed; if any `delivery.mode` references survive, this is the catch-all.
- SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md: ditto.

### CHANGELOG / release notes

Per project_changelog_auto memory: don't hand-curate `[Unreleased]`. Instead:
- Audit recent commit bodies for richness — every bl-XXXX merge in the 0.5.0 window must have a body paragraph explaining 'why', because release-plz reads bodies to generate `[Unreleased]`.
- Draft a separate `docs/RELEASE-NOTES-0.5.0.md` (or equivalent) that frames the release at the human level: 'three-file XDG layout is the new default', 'how to migrate', 'min_bl_version is now X.Y.Z', 'recent big features shipped alongside (8a9a/e609/ffb4/1f38/32e5/b017/2715/d4b0)'.
- This release-notes doc is what the user references when announcing or when an old clone hits the version-floor warning. release-plz still owns the `CHANGELOG.md` entry.

## Exit criteria

- Fresh reader of README + SKILL.md + SPEC-clone-layout could install `bl`, run `bl init` on a brand-new repo, and end up with an XDG layout without consulting source code.
- No grep hit for `delivery.mode`, `review.pre_check`, `workspace.json` outside §11 migration sections.
- Release notes draft committed to a branch; review-ready.

## Why a discrete ball

Documentation written *during* implementation phases consistently drifts — a phase ships, the next phase's SPEC changes a name, the README quietly desyncs. A consolidated post-implementation sweep with the *whole* shipped reality in front of it produces docs that match what users will actually encounter. The 0.5.0 release-notes draft is the natural pair: it can't be written until the integration sweep (Phase 6) confirms what the release actually delivers.

## Risks

- The 'no `workspace.json` references outside §11' grep is easy to bypass with paraphrase. Pair it with a second check: any code or doc reference to a *file path* under `.balls/` on the integration branch outside of migration text. Failing this means the writer mentally still operated in the old layout.
title: Phase 7: documentation final pass + 0.5.0 release notes
